title: Two Ancient 3090s vs a $9,000 Blackwell Card
excerpt: I benchmarked my dual-3090 rig against a pair of RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell cards. The five-year-old hardware is ~60% as fast as a $9,000 GPU on decode — until you ask it to do prefill, or load a model that doesn't fit in 48GB.

title: If Claude Fable stops helping you, you'll never know
excerpt: Anthropic's Claude Fable policy turns coding assistants into non-neutral infrastructure for product companies.

title: My Local LLM Rig (draft)
excerpt: The hardware and software stack behind training and serving open-weights models from my home office — 2x 3090s, 256GB of non-ECC RAM, and a Threadripper that's older than it should be.

title: Claude Code is a Footgun
excerpt: How agentic coding tools can build impressive features while making your codebase incomprehensible.

title: Alignment Makes AI Less Human
excerpt: A base model trained on my text history showed me relationship patterns that therapy couldn't.

title: I Built a Geocoder for AI Agents Because I Couldn't Afford Google Maps
excerpt: The VC-backed startups got Google Cloud credits. I got Photon, OpenStreetMap, and open weights embedding models. The scraps turned out to be enough.

title: Everyone in Seattle Hates AI
excerpt: How Microsoft's AI strategy turned brilliant engineers against the technology they should be building.

title: Visualizing bsdiff: The Delta Compression Algorithm Used by macOS and Google Chrome
excerpt: An interactive exploration of the bsdiff binary diff algorithm used for software updates in macOS and Chrome.
